root_text.an11.9:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.an11.9:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā saddho yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ saddhaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an11.9:2.4: Assakhaḷuṅko hi, saddha, doṇiyā baddho ‘yavasaṁ yavasan’ti jhāyati.
root_text.an11.9:2.6: Na hi, saddha, assakhaḷuṅkassa doṇiyā baddhassa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an11.9:2.7: ‘kiṁ nu kho maṁ ajja assadammasārathi kāraṇaṁ kāressati, kimassāhaṁ paṭikaromī’ti.
root_text.an11.9:2.9: Evamevaṁ kho, saddha, idhekacco purisakhaḷuṅko araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati kāmarāgaparetena uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.9:2.10: So kāmarāgaṁyeva antaraṁ katvā jhāyati pajjhāyati nijjhāyati avajjhāyati,
root_text.an11.9:2.14: vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an11.9:2.15: So vicikicchaṁyeva antaraṁ katvā jhāyati pajjhāyati nijjhāyati avajjhāyati.
root_text.an11.9:2.16: So pathavimpi nissāya jhāyati, āpampi nissāya jhāyati, tejampi nissāya jhāyati, vāyampi nissāya jhāyati, ākāsānañcāyatanampi nissāya jhāyati, viññāṇañcāyatanampi nissāya jhāyati, ākiñcaññāyatanampi nissāya jhāyati, nevasaññānāsaññāyatanampi nissāya jhāyati, idhalokampi nissāya jhāyati, paralokampi nissāya jhāyati, yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tampi nissāya jhāyati.
root_text.an11.9:3.2: Bhadro hi, saddha, assājānīyo doṇiyā baddho na ‘yavasaṁ yavasan’ti jhāyati.
root_text.an11.9:3.4: Bhadrassa hi, saddha, assājānīyassa doṇiyā baddhassa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an11.9:3.5: ‘kiṁ nu kho maṁ ajja assadammasārathi kāraṇaṁ kāressati, kimassāhaṁ paṭikaromī’ti.
root_text.an11.9:3.6: So doṇiyā baddho na ‘yavasaṁ yavasan’ti jhāyati.
root_text.an11.9:3.7: Bhadro hi, saddha, assājānīyo yathā iṇaṁ yathā bandhaṁ yathā jāniṁ yathā kaliṁ evaṁ patodassa ajjhoharaṇaṁ samanupassati.
root_text.an11.9:3.8: Evamevaṁ kho, saddha, bhadro purisājānīyo araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi na kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti,
root_text.an11.9:3.12: na vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an11.9:3.13: So neva pathaviṁ nissāya jhāyati, na āpaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na tejaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na vāyaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na idhalokaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na paralokaṁ nissāya jhāyati, yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tampi nissāya na jhāyati;
root_text.an11.9:3.15: Evaṁ jhāyiñca pana, saddha, bhadraṁ purisājānīyaṁ saindā devā sabrahmakā sapajāpatikā ārakāva namassanti:
root_text.an11.9:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā saddho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an11.9:5.2: “kathaṁ jhāyī pana, bhante, bhadro purisājānīyo neva pathaviṁ nissāya jhāyati, na āpaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na tejaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na vāyaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na idhalokaṁ nissāya jhāyati, na paralokaṁ nissāya jhāyati, yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tampi nissāya na jhāyati;
root_text.an11.9:5.4: Kathaṁ jhāyiñca pana, bhante, bhadraṁ purisājānīyaṁ saindā devā sabrahmakā sapajāpatikā ārakāva namassanti:
root_text.an11.9:7.1: “Idha, saddha, bhadrassa purisājānīyassa pathaviyaṁ pathavisaññā vibhūtā hoti, āpasmiṁ āposaññā vibhūtā hoti, tejasmiṁ tejosaññā vibhūtā hoti, vāyasmiṁ vāyosaññā vibhūtā hoti, ākāsānañcāyatane ākāsānañcāyatanasaññā vibhūtā hoti, viññāṇañcāyatane viññāṇañcāyatanasaññā vibhūtā hoti, ākiñcaññāyatane ākiñcaññāyatanasaññā vibhūtā hoti, nevasaññānāsaññāyatane nevasaññānāsaññāyatanasaññā vibhūtā hoti, idhaloke idhalokasaññā vibhūtā hoti, paraloke paralokasaññā vibhūtā hoti, yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tatrāpi saññā vibhūtā hoti.
root_text.an11.9:7.2: Evaṁ jhāyī kho, saddha, bhadro purisājānīyo neva pathaviṁ nissāya jhāyati …pe…
root_text.an11.9:7.3: yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tampi nissāya na jhāyati;
root_text.an11.9:7.5: Evaṁ jhāyiñca pana, saddha, bhadraṁ purisājānīyaṁ saindā devā sabrahmakā sapajāpatikā ārakāva namassanti:

translation_text.an11.9:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.an11.9:1.2: Then Venerable Sandha went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an11.9:2.3: And how does a wild colt meditate?
translation_text.an11.9:2.4: A wild colt, tied up by the feeding trough, meditates: ‘Fodder, fodder!’
translation_text.an11.9:2.6: Because it doesn’t occur to the wild colt tied up by the feeding trough:
translation_text.an11.9:2.7: ‘What task will the horse trainer have me do today? How should I respond?’
translation_text.an11.9:2.8: Tied up by the feeding trough they just meditate: ‘Fodder, fodder!’
translation_text.an11.9:2.9: In the same way, take a certain wild person who has gone to the forest, the root of a tree, or an empty hut. Their heart is overcome and mired in sensual desire, and they don’t truly understand the escape from sensual desire that has arisen.
translation_text.an11.9:2.10: Harboring sensual desire within they meditate and concentrate and contemplate and ruminate.
translation_text.an11.9:2.11: Their heart is overcome by ill will …
translation_text.an11.9:2.15: Harboring doubt within they meditate and concentrate and contemplate and ruminate.
translation_text.an11.9:2.16: They meditate dependent on earth, water, fire, and air. They meditate dependent on the dimension of infinite space, infinite consciousness, nothingness, or neither perception nor non-perception. They meditate dependent on this world or the other world. They meditate dependent on what is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, or explored by the mind.
translation_text.an11.9:2.17: That’s how a wild colt meditates.
translation_text.an11.9:3.1: And how does a thoroughbred meditate?
translation_text.an11.9:3.2: A fine thoroughbred, tied up by the feeding trough, doesn’t meditate: ‘Fodder, fodder!’
translation_text.an11.9:3.4: Because it occurs to the fine thoroughbred tied up by the feeding trough:
translation_text.an11.9:3.5: ‘What task will the horse trainer have me do today? How should I respond?’
translation_text.an11.9:3.6: Tied up by the feeding trough they don’t meditate: ‘Fodder, fodder!’
translation_text.an11.9:3.7: For that fine thoroughbred regards the use of the goad as a debt, a bond, a loss, a misfortune.
translation_text.an11.9:3.8: In the same way, take a certain fine thoroughbred person who has gone to the forest, the root of a tree, or an empty hut. Their heart is not overcome and mired in sensual desire, and they truly understand the escape from sensual desire that has arisen.
translation_text.an11.9:3.9: Their heart is not overcome by ill will …
translation_text.an11.9:3.13: They don’t meditate dependent on earth, water, fire, and air. They don’t meditate dependent on the dimension of infinite space, infinite consciousness, nothingness, or neither perception nor non-perception. They don’t meditate dependent on this world or the other world. They don’t meditate dependent on what is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, or explored by the mind.
translation_text.an11.9:3.15: When a fine thoroughbred meditates like this, the gods together with Indra, the Divinity, and the Progenitor worship them from afar:
translation_text.an11.9:4.2: Homage to you, supreme among men!
translation_text.an11.9:5.1: When he said this, Venerable Sandha asked the Buddha,
translation_text.an11.9:5.2: “But sir, how does that fine thoroughbred meditate?”
translation_text.an11.9:7.1: “Sandha, for a fine thoroughbred person, the perception of earth has vanished in relation to earth. The perception of water … fire … air has vanished in relation to air. The perception of the dimension of infinite space has vanished in relation to the dimension of infinite space. The perception of the dimension of infinite consciousness … nothingness … neither perception nor non-perception has vanished in relation to the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception. The perception of this world has vanished in relation to this world. The perception of the other world has vanished in relation to the other world. And the perception of what is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, or explored by the mind has vanished.
translation_text.an11.9:7.2: That’s how that fine thoroughbred person doesn’t meditate dependent on earth, water, fire, and air. They don’t meditate dependent on the dimension of infinite space, infinite consciousness, nothingness, or neither perception nor non-perception. They don’t meditate dependent on this world or the other world.
translation_text.an11.9:7.3: They don’t meditate dependent on what is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, or explored by the mind.
translation_text.an11.9:7.5: When a fine thoroughbred person meditates like this, the gods together with Indra, the Divinity, and the Progenitor worship them from afar:
translation_text.an11.9:8.2: Homage to you, supreme among men!
